title: announce: No rate limiting on signature verification - CPU exhaustion vector
description:
File: python/src/lichen/announce/processor.py:169-179

The processor performs signature verification on EVERY incoming announce before checking the seen table:

```python
# Step 1: Verify IID matches pubkey hash (cheap)
expected_iid = _pubkey_to_iid(announce.pubkey)
...
# Step 2: Verify signature (EXPENSIVE - crypto operation)
signable = announce.signed_data()
if not verify(announce.pubkey, signable, announce.signature):
    ...
# Step 4: Check for stale/duplicate (cheap, could be done earlier)
existing_seq = self._seen.get(iid)
```

An attacker can flood the node with announces containing:
1. Valid IID/pubkey pairs (their own keys or generated ones)
2. Invalid signatures

Each announce triggers a full signature verification (expensive crypto) before being rejected.

Mitigations to consider:
1. Rate-limit announce processing per source IP
2. Check seen table BEFORE signature verification for known originators
3. Implement a 'recently seen invalid signature' cache to fast-reject repeat offenders

Note: The IID derivation check (Step 1) is cheap and does run first, providing some protection.
close_reason: Implemented early stale/seen check before signature verification to mitigate CPU exhaustion from invalid announces. Tests and ruff pass. (local commit 79fb52aa6)
